Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Aaron Gorfein of Law Offices of Elizabeth F. McDonald telephone number 415-512-7788 for Plaintiff Rashmi Gupta.
John Shupe of Lynch and Shupe, LLP telephone number 650-579-5950 for Defendant San Francisco State University.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication is denied in their entirety. Defendant maintains its initial burden of production. The declarations of Taylor, Corrigan, Rosser, and Wong show that the decision to deny tenure was not the result of discriminatory or retaliatory animus. (Taylor Dec., 2:13-21; 3:5-14; 4:1-17; 5:1-12; 5:13-6:6; 6:13-27; 7:3-8, Wong Dec., 1:28-3:5, Corrigan Dec., 1:28-2:2; Rosser Dec., 2:1-14; 4:1-5:18; 6:2-17.) However, Dr. Gupta creates a triable issue whether defendant's purported legitimate reason for denying her tenure to plaintiff was pretextual and whether similarly situated persons were treated more favorably. The criteria to determine teaching effectiveness is multi-faceted and no one criterion prevails over others. (Defendant's Evidence Ex. G). The reviewing body should take account of the difficulty of the class when assessing the reviews of a teacher. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Ex. 9, pg. 7 of Hermoso Report). Dean Taylor considered the difficulty of classes when assessing other associate professors. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Ex. 9, 1/25/13 Taylor letter). When assessing Dr. Gupta, there is evidence that Dean Taylor did not follow the applicable criteria and gave little consideration regarding the difficulty of the classes taught by Dr. Gupta. (Plaintiff's Evidence Ex. 7, Taylor Depo., 32:25-34:1, 45:7-46:11, 56:3-23, 58:3-59:17, 70:3-9,95:4-97:2). A reasonable trier of fact could also conclude that Dr. Gupta's teaching scores were not evenly compared with Ms. Hermoso's scores. (Plaintiff's Ex. 14, Plaintiff's Report pgs. 4-7 attached to DeVries Dec. and Ex. 9, pgs. 5-7 of Hermoso Report). Dean Taylor stated that Dr. Gupta did not have 10 positive peer reviews (Plaintiff's Evidence Ex. 7, Taylor Depo., 71:4-8), but other evidence suggests that she did. (Plaintiff's Evidence Ex. 14, Plaintiff's Report pg. 3 attached to DeVries Dec.). There is also evidence that Dean Taylor was upset with Dr. Gupta after she complained about a co-worker. (Plaintiff's Evidence Ex. 14, DeVries Dec., par. 3; Plaintiff's Evidence Ex. 3, Gupta Depo., 312:1-14; 318:1-3). A reasonable trier of fact could find that defendant's proffered reason for the denial of tenure is not worthy of credence and the true reason for that decision was discrimination and/or retaliation and thus defendant is not entitled to summary adjudication on issues 1 and 2. Defendant is not entitled to summary adjudication on issues 3-6 because those issues are not directed to the entirety of a cause of action as required by CCP 437c(f)(1). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
